2605.17017.title: When Dynamics Shift, Robust Task Inference Wins: Offline Imitation Learning with Behavior Foundation Models Revisited
2605.17017.paper_summary: Behavior Foundation Models (BFMs) enable scalable imitation learning (IL) by pretraining task-agnostic representations that can be rapidly adapted to new tasks. However, existing BFMs assume fixed environment dynamics, limiting their robustness under real-world shifts such as changes in friction, actuation, or sensor noise. We address this by formulating BFM task-inference as a robust minimax optimization problem, enabling adaptation to worst-case dynamics perturbations without modifying pretraining. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first BFM-based framework that achieves robustness to dynamics shifts while relying solely on offline data from a single nominal environment. Our approach significantly outperforms standard BFM and robust offline IL baselines under dynamics shifts. These results demonstrate that robust policy can be achieved entirely at task-inference time, improving the practicality of BFMs in dynamic settings.
2605.17017.paper_summary_zh: 行為基礎模型（BFMs）透過預訓練任務無關的表示，使可擴展的模仿學習（IL）成為可能，這些表示可以迅速適應新任務。然而，現有的BFMs假設環境動態是固定的，這限制了它們在現實世界變化下的穩健性，例如摩擦、驅動或傳感器噪聲的變化。我們通過將BFM任務推斷公式化為一個穩健的最小最大優化問題來解決這一問題，使其能夠適應最壞情況下的動態擾動，而無需修改預訓練。據我們所知，這是第一個基於BFM的框架，實現了對動態變化的穩健性，同時僅依賴於來自單一名義環境的離線數據。我們的方法在動態變化下顯著超越了標準BFM和穩健的離線IL基線。這些結果證明，穩健的策略可以完全在任務推斷時實現，從而提高BFMs在動態環境中的實用性。
2605.17017.author: Rishabh Agrawal et.al.
2605.17017.authors: Rishabh Agrawal, Rahul Jain, Ashutosh Nayyar
